paper_title: CRISPR-Cas9 Mutagenesis and TIDE Sequencing Analysis in Phaeodactylum

methods.description: TIDE (Tracking of Indels by DEconvolution) sequence analysis is an online-based software used to analyze raw sequencing data to find indel mutations at a Cas9 target site. It was used to analyze indels resulting from Cas9 targeting of nitrate reductase in Phaeodactylum.

steps.description: 80 colonies for gNR-A and 64 colonies for gNR-B were picked directly into PCR mix.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare PCR mix with colonies for sequencing

steps.description: 200 ng of the PCR amplicons were then directly sequenced using two primers, NR-HRM-A and NR-gene-2, for paired-end reads.
steps.searchable_summary: sequence PCR amplicons using paired-end reads

steps.description: TIDE analysis requires the input of (1) the nuclease species used (SpCas9), (2) the 20 nt spacer target, (3) an ABI (.abi) file for a reference sequence (control Cas9-2A-sh ble), and (4) an ABI (.abi) file for a sample sequence.
steps.searchable_summary: input data into TIDE for analysis

steps.description: Certain criteria were identified and selected for when analyzing the TIDE output: total efficiency value of 50%, p-value <0.001, and distinct genotypes with predominant indels.
steps.searchable_summary: validate TIDE output based on efficiency, p-value, and genotype criteria

steps.description: For cell lines that passed criteria, results were verified by conducting TIDE analysis of the paired-end read of the same sample.
steps.searchable_summary: verify results with paired-end TIDE analysis

methods.description: Workflow for episomal exchange to Phaeodactylum colony genotyping using a selectable Cas9 episome. This includes episome construction, transformation, and genotyping of mutant cell lines.

steps.description: The Phaeodactylum episome was built with a transcriptionally fused Cas9 and sh ble, an antibiotic selectable marker gene.
steps.searchable_summary: construct episome with Cas9 and selectable marker

steps.description: A hierarchical cloning strategy was used to build and clone one Phaeodactylum sgRNA expression cassette in two golden-gate assembly steps.
steps.searchable_summary: clone sgRNA expression cassette using golden-gate assembly

steps.description: After construction and sequence verification, a Cas9-sh ble:sgRNA episome was transformed into electro-competent E. coli cells.
steps.searchable_summary: transform episome into E. coli cells

steps.description: The episome then resides in the Phaeodactylum nucleus, separate from the chromosomes, where expression of Cas9 and sgRNA(s) occurs.
steps.searchable_summary: express Cas9 and sgRNA in Phaeodactylum nucleus

steps.description: Colonies were first picked and added directly to PCR mix to amplify the sgRNA expression cassette on the episome.
steps.searchable_summary: amplify sgRNA expression cassette via PCR

steps.description: Only colonies that contained a correctly sized amplicon were chosen for genotyping.
steps.searchable_summary: select colonies with correct amplicon size for genotyping
